data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 4. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-6f73d300">
    <title>spec: define needs-human operator review lane</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx lacks a first-class operator-attention lane for beads that need human review. A Codex/Claude TUI or the web GUI cannot reliably answer “show me beads needing my attention” and then capture the operator review decision.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 defines ready/status as open/ready/blocked counts only. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:223-229 defines Ready queue view but no Needs review filter. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md:243-263 defines review actions but not needs_human review intake/resolution in the bead GUI. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:343-352 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:156-170 say terminal review cases block with needs_human but do not define status, metadata, CLI/TUI discovery, GUI expression, or resolution semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
Amend FEAT-004, FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and ADR-024 to define needs_human as status=open plus needs_human label and structured reason metadata; skipped by workers; discoverable through CLI/TUI and a Beads list Needs review filter; resolvable through explicit retry/split/obsolete/defer review actions with required notes in CLI and web detail UI.

NON-SCOPE
Implementation of CLI, GraphQL, web, or worker changes. New bd/br status values. Full artifact-review chat UI.

DEPS
No deps; this is the prerequisite spec contract.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md defines needs_human lane state, metadata keys, worker-skip semantics, and ready/status count behavior.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md adds user stories for a Beads list Needs review filter and a bead detail review-resolution panel.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md specifies terminal review needs_human unclaims while retry/escalate review blocks preserve retry behavior.
4. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md aligns review-manual-required and review_spec_gap with operator-attention lane resolution.
5. Spec names metadata keys needs-human-reason, needs-human-since, needs-human-source, needs-human-suggested-action, and needs-human-summary.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:specs, area:web, area:cli, kind:doc, reliability, operator-ux, needs-human-lane, adr:024, spec:FEAT-004, spec:FEAT-008, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ac10e7af" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="82a404038fad97dda63524aa5bb367b27eb72d8a" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T024328-79086042"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `f4df6eb79` with subject `docs: define needs-human operator review lane [ddx-6f73d300]`.

- FEAT-004 now defines the operator-attention lane, including the `needs_human` label, the `needs-human-*` metadata keys, and ready/status behavior in [`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md#L39 ) and [`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md#L80 ).
- FEAT-008 adds the `Needs review` list filter and a bead-detail review-resolution user story in [`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md#L205 ) and [`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md#L658 ).
- FEAT-010 now distinguishes retryable review fixes from terminal `needs_human` cases and explicitly clears claim state for the terminal path in [`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md#L343 ).
- ADR-024 now aligns `review_spec_gap` and `review-manual-required` with the same operator-attention lane resolution in [`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md#L156 ) and [`docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md`]( /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-6f73d300-20260507T024328-79086042/docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md#L168 ).
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.
